contributingFactors: emptyDir volume sizeLimit (8Gi) is nearly equal to the total available filesystem size (~8.15 GiB free on a 10.46 GiB FS), leaving no headroom
PostgreSQL initialization generated a sustained disk write spike peaking at ~167 KB/s (~9x baseline), consuming ~1.44 GiB in 10 minutes
No PersistentVolumeClaim — all PostgreSQL data stored ephemerally on node-local storage, directly consuming kubelet filesystem space
Node kubelet filesystem (/var/lib/kubelet on /dev/loop1) has only ~7.9 GiB allocatable ephemeral storage per node capacity
predict_linear over 10-minute window projects available bytes at ~4.9 GiB in 30 minutes if write rate continues
rootCauseAnalysis.summary: PostgreSQL Deployment 'postgres-emptydir' uses an emptyDir volume with an 8Gi sizeLimit that writes directly to node dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com's /var/lib/kubelet filesystem (/dev/loop1, 10.46 GiB total), causing disk consumption that triggered the predict_linear DiskPressure alert. Available space dropped ~1.44 GiB in 10 minutes during PostgreSQL initialization, and predict_linear projects ~4.9 GiB free in 30 minutes if write rates continue. The root fix is migrating from ephemeral emptyDir to a PersistentVolumeClaim backed by TopoLVM/LVMS.

selectedWorkflow.rationale: This workflow is purpose-built for the exact scenario: DiskPressure caused by emptyDir growth from a stateful database workload managed by ArgoCD/GitOps. All preconditions are satisfied — the namespace is ArgoCD-managed (gitOpsManaged=true, gitOpsTool=argocd), the pod uses emptyDir for PostgreSQL data, and the cluster has TopoLVM/LVMS StorageClass available for PVC provisioning. The workflow performs a live pg_dump backup, commits the emptyDir→PVC manifest change to Git, waits for ArgoCD sync, and restores the database — the correct proactive remediation before DiskPressure materializes.

causalChain: Signal: predict_linear on /var/lib/kubelet filesystem predicts disk exhaustion within 30 minutes on dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com
Why? Available space dropped from 9.59 GiB to 8.15 GiB (~1.44 GiB consumed) in 10 minutes starting at 15:54Z
Why? PostgreSQL pod postgres-emptydir-69679b77bb-frhq4 was deployed at 15:54:57Z and its initialization generated a disk write spike peaking at 167 KB/s on /dev/vda
Why? The Deployment 'postgres-emptydir' mounts an emptyDir volume (sizeLimit: 8Gi) for PostgreSQL data storage, which writes directly to the node's /var/lib/kubelet filesystem

Root cause: Deployment 'postgres-emptydir' uses ephemeral emptyDir storage (8Gi limit) for a stateful PostgreSQL workload on a node with only 10.46 GiB total kubelet filesystem space, with no PersistentVolumeClaim to offload data to dedicated storage — the emptyDir capacity nearly equals the entire available node filesystem

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/postgres-emptydir because its volume specification (emptyDir instead of PVC) is the configuration that needs to change. Changing this to a PVC would redirect PostgreSQL writes to dedicated storage (TopoLVM is available), removing the kubelet filesystem pressure. The Node is the symptom reporter, not the fix target.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment in demo-diskpressure namespace is the direct cause. The fix (replacing emptyDir with PVC) is targeted and well-scoped. TopoLVM is already provisioned on this node, making PVC migration straightforward.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: node conditions, all pods in demo-diskpressure namespace, Prometheus filesystem metrics (avail, size, predict_linear), disk write rate trends, container filesystem usage, pod events and logs (current and previous), Deployment spec, node storage configuration (TopoLVM). Did not investigate: other namespaces' pods on the same node for additional disk consumers (container_fs_usage_bytes showed no other significant consumers), ConfigMap postgres-init-sql content (not relevant to disk pressure root cause).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from alert signal (predict_linear) → observed disk drop (1.44 GiB in 10 min) → correlated write spike with pod deployment timestamp → identified emptyDir as the storage mechanism consuming kubelet filesystem space → identified missing PVC as the root configuration gap. Cannot go deeper without application-level write pattern analysis.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims backed by tool evidence: disk drop confirmed by Prometheus range query (9.59→8.15 GiB), write spike confirmed by node_disk_written_bytes_total range query (18→167 KB/s), pod deployment time correlated with disk drop onset, emptyDir configuration confirmed by kubectl Deployment spec inspection, TopoLVM presence confirmed by node describe output (finalizer topolvm.io/node, volume groups vg1/00default). predict_linear projection confirmed at ~4.9 GiB in 30 minutes. No inaccessible data sources.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/postgres-emptydir or Node/dev-worker-0.redhat-internal.com (both tier1 and tier2 history are empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: Considered: (1) Other workloads causing disk consumption — ruled out: container_fs_usage_bytes shows only 20480 bytes for demo-diskpressure pod, other overlay devices show identical values suggesting no other burst; (2) System-level disk consumption (etcd, logs) — ruled out: write spike is isolated to vda and correlates precisely with pod deployment; (3) False positive / transient spike — partially ruled out: disk has stabilized at 8.15 GiB but the emptyDir 8Gi limit means PostgreSQL growth will continue to consume space; (4) Node DiskPressure already active — confirmed False per node condition.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.88: Starting at 1.0. -0.05 for disk writes having stabilized at time of investigation (prediction may not fully materialize if PostgreSQL data growth is slow); -0.05 for not being able to confirm exact PostgreSQL data growth rate going forward; -0.02 for container_fs_usage_bytes showing only 20KB for the pod (cadvisor may not be capturing emptyDir usage correctly, though node_filesystem metrics confirm the overall drop).

contributingFactors: emptyDir volume sizeLimit (8Gi) is nearly equal to the total available filesystem size (~8.15 GiB free on a 10.46 GiB FS), leaving no headroom
PostgreSQL initialization generated a sustained disk write spike peaking at ~167 KB/s (~9x baseline), consuming ~1.44 GiB in 10 minutes
No PersistentVolumeClaim — all PostgreSQL data stored ephemerally on node-local storage, directly consuming kubelet filesystem space
Node kubelet filesystem (/var/lib/kubelet on /dev/loop1) has only ~7.9 GiB allocatable ephemeral storage per node capacity
predict_linear over 10-minute window projects available bytes at ~4.9 GiB in 30 minutes if write rate continues
